doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_inits2 (mpfr/src/inits2.c L40-L64). C signature is varargs with leading prec: mpfr_inits2(prec, x, y, z, ..., (mpfr_ptr)0) -- iterate, initialize each via mpfr_init2 at the supplied prec, terminate on the NULL sentinel. C mutates in place and returns void.

Sister of mpfr_inits with an explicit prec. Same TS surface decision: takes (prec: bigint, n: bigint) and returns n: bigint (count-passthrough success marker). The prec is validated against PREC_MIN / PREC_MAX (same bounds as mpfr_init2).

Class 'misc' (1000ms). Tag minimums per Rule 7: happy 20, edge 30, adv 12, fuzz 50, mined 5 (or fewer from mpfr/tests/tinits.c).
c_signature: void mpfr_inits2(mpfr_prec_t p, mpfr_ptr x, ... /* mpfr_ptr 0 sentinel */)
divergence_from_c:
Five divergences:

  1. Parameter shape: C (prec, varargs of mpfr_ptr terminated by NULL) -> TS (prec: bigint, n: bigint count).

  2. Return type: C void -> TS bigint (count-passthrough success marker; mirrors mpfr_inits and mpfr_free_pool patterns).

  3. Allocation semantics: C allocates limb storage and leaves mantissa uninitialised; TS allocates JS bigints implicitly. User-side composition `Array.from({length: Number(n)}, () => mpfr_init2(prec))` covers this if the caller needs actual values.

  4. Precision validation: TS throws MPFRError('EPREC') for prec out of [PREC_MIN, PREC_MAX]; C returns void and (in debug builds) ASSERTs.

  5. Count validation: TS rejects n < 0; C has no analog (its varargs walk terminates on the NULL sentinel regardless).
refs: mpfr/src/inits2.c L40-L64 -- the C reference body.
src/ops/init2.ts -- the underlying single-handle init2 (explicit prec); composing init2(prec) in a loop is the user-side analogue.
mpfr/tests/tinits.c L33 -- mined-tests source (mpfr_inits2(200, a, b, c, NULL) pattern).
src/core.ts -- locked schema (PREC_MIN, PREC_MAX, MPFR shape).
eval/functions/mpfr_free_pool/spec.json -- precedent: void-returning op encoded as success-marker scalar.
